summary: Backend testing of Social Media module (Agorapulse-style refactoring). All 20 tests PASSED (100% success rate). Fixed MongoDB ObjectId serialization bug in social_media.py router.
critical_code_review_comments: Fixed: MongoDB _id ObjectId serialization bug in /app/backend/routes/social_media.py - added entity.pop('_id', None) after insert_one operations in create_entity, create_social_account, create_post, and create_template functions
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up TEST_ prefixed posts and entities during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Social Media module fully tested. Two endpoint systems exist: 1) Original /api/social/posts in server.py (ScheduledPost model) - used for basic post scheduling. 2) New multi-entity system in routes/social_media.py with /api/social/entities, /api/social/accounts, /api/social/posts (SocialPostCreate model), /api/social/queue, /api/social/capabilities, /api/social/stats. The original endpoint in server.py takes precedence for /api/social/posts due to router registration order.

GET /api/social/entities.returns: list of entities with account_count and account_ids

POST /api/social/entities.returns: created entity with id, name, color, account_count=0

GET /api/social/accounts.returns: list of accounts without encrypted tokens

POST /api/social/sync-meta-accounts.returns: 404 if no Meta account connected, 200 with synced count otherwise

bug_fixed.issue: MongoDB ObjectId serialization error in social_media.py router
bug_fixed.root_cause: After insert_one(), MongoDB adds _id field to the document. When returning the document directly, FastAPI's jsonable_encoder fails to serialize ObjectId
bug_fixed.fix: Added entity.pop('_id', None) after insert_one() in create_entity, create_social_account, create_post, and create_template functions
